reasoning: The most recent 10-year Treasury yield is 4.40%, with a clear near-term downward trend over the past month (4.51 -> 4.44 -> 4.44 -> 4.43 -> 4.40). The FRED statistical model predicts a 0.39-0.45 probability of a near-term increase. Long term forecasts are mixed but generally flat: a March Reuters poll forecasted 4.45% at 6 months and 1 year out, while the CBO's longer-term forecast sees yielding drifting lower towards 4.1% average for the year. Balancing the downward short-term momentum with long-term reversion to random walk behavior (p=0.5), I set the 1-week probability at 0.41, gradually rising to 0.48 for the 6-month horizon as uncertainty increases and the market expectation of slight positive carry in the forward curve offsets the downside risks.
